content.text: Found a **real #1062 regression**: `TemplateSyntaxError: Encountered unknown tag 'endblock'... expecting 'endif'`. My #1062 added the two backfill cards wrapped in `{% if is_admin %}` but **didn't close it with `{% endif %}`** — breaking the entire dependency-map page (500). All 6 `dependency_map_routes` failures are this one unclosed-`{% if %}`. The review approved the frontend but the existing page-render tests weren't run. Templates are editable in main context — let me find and fix it.

echo "=== if/endif/endblock balance in dependency_map.html ==="
grep -nE "\{% *if |\{% *endif|\{% *block|\{% *endblock|backfill-panel" src/code_indexer/server/web/templates/dependency_map.html | tail -30

content.text: The `{% if is_admin %}` at line 297 is **redundant** — line 10's `{% if is_admin %}` is still open (no `{% endif %}` between 10 and 297), so the cards at 297-341 are already admin-gated. #1062 added a nested `{% if %}` without a closing `{% endif %}`, causing the imbalance. Removing the redundant line 297 restores balance and keeps the cards admin-gated. (Templates are editable in main context.)

input.old_string:
{% if is_admin %}
<!-- Story #1062: Lifecycle Backfill Journal Panel -->
<!-- Starts display:none; JS shows it when X-Backfill-Active=1; hides after server-side grace -->
<article id="lifecycle-backfill-panel" style="display: none; margin-top: 1rem;">
input.new_string:
<!-- Story #1062: Lifecycle Backfill Journal Panel -->
<!-- Starts display:none; JS shows it when X-Backfill-Active=1; hides after server-side grace -->
<!-- Already inside the outer {% if is_admin %} block (admin-gated); no nested if needed -->
<article id="lifecycle-backfill-panel" style="display: none; margin-top: 1rem;">
